entry.title: Legal information
entry.subtitle: Open the privacy policy and the licenses and third-party materials pages separately.

hero.title: Privacy policy
hero.subtitle: This document describes what data Memicard processes, for what purposes, to whom it may be disclosed, how long it is stored, and what rights the user has.

overview.title: Policy scope
overview.body: Memicard stores most data locally on the user's device as a general rule. This policy covers data stored by the app, data voluntarily provided by the user, and information related to optional device features used by the app.
bullets: the app does not require a user account for its basic operation
the app does not use its own backend for ongoing user data handling
this policy reflects the current state of the app as of March 9, 2026

controller.title: Data controller and contact
controller.body: The data controller for personal data processed in connection with the use of Memicard is [Controller full name]. Privacy contact: contact@memicard.com.
bullets: optional registered or correspondence address: [Registered address]
the technical support email address may be different from the privacy contact address
before publishing this policy, the placeholders should be replaced with the controller's real details

purposes.title: Purposes and legal bases
purposes.body: Data is processed only to the extent necessary for the app to work or to respond to the user's request.
bullets: local data is processed to operate the app, store progress, statistics, settings, and user-created content, on the basis of providing the service or taking steps requested by the user
local study reminders are enabled only after the user gives permission
data import and export, file selection, and image selection happen only at the user's request
data sent by email is processed to handle the request, reply to the message, diagnose an issue, and ensure security, on the basis of legitimate interest or steps taken at the user's request

localData.title: Categories of data processed locally
localData.body: The app stores application data and user content locally when needed for learning and app features.
bullets: learning progress, statistics, review state, and support data used by the learning mechanics
pinned courses, custom courses, custom flashcards, and settings related to editing them
app settings, interface preferences, and helper states saved by the app
if the user enters personal data into their own flashcards, courses, or descriptions, that data is also stored locally on the device
localData.note: Data is stored locally mainly with SQLite and AsyncStorage.

permissions.title: Permissions and device features
permissions.body: Memicard uses device features only when they are needed for a selected feature.
bullets: local notifications for study reminders are used only after the user grants permission
file access is used for data import, export, and user data copies
system sharing may be used when exporting data outside the app
the app may open the default mail client to prepare a support message
the app requests access to the photo library only when the user wants to add their own image to a flashcard

recipients.title: Recipients and third parties
recipients.body: As a rule, Memicard does not send user data to the developer's servers for normal app operation.
bullets: data stays on the user's device unless the user chooses to export it, share it, or send it by email
when the user contacts support by email, data may be processed by the user's email provider and system mail services
when the user exports or shares files, data may be stored outside the app according to the service, location, or app chosen by the user
based on the current state of the app, no third-party advertising SDKs or cross-app tracking systems are used

retention.title: Retention period
retention.body: The storage period depends on the type of data and how the app is used.
bullets: local data is stored until the user deletes it, resets the app, removes selected content, or removes the app
data sent by email is stored for the time needed to handle the request and is then deleted or anonymized, generally for no longer than 12 months, unless the law requires longer retention
files exported outside the app remain outside Memicard's control and are stored until deleted by the user or by the selected service provider

noTracking.title: What the app does not do
noTracking.body: According to the current state of the app as of March 9, 2026, Memicard:
bullets: does not sell user data
does not use behavioral advertising
does not track activity across apps
does not send user data to its own server for normal app operation

deletion.title: Data deletion
deletion.body: The user keeps control over data stored by the app on the device.
bullets: some data can be removed inside the app, for example by deleting custom courses, custom flashcards, or resetting selected settings
removing the app generally removes its local data from the device, subject to backup or restore mechanisms offered by the operating system or user services
data copies exported to files or external services remain outside the app and are managed by the user

transfers.title: Transfers outside the EU or EEA
transfers.body: As a rule, Memicard does not transfer user data to its own servers outside the European Economic Area.
bullets: when the user contacts support by email or uses third-party services chosen by the user, data may be processed under those providers' own rules
the scope and location of processing by an external provider depends on the service selected by the user, and Memicard does not control those providers' privacy policies

security.title: Data security
security.body: The app follows a data minimization approach and uses system permissions only when needed for a selected feature.
bullets: local data stays on the user's device unless the user chooses to export or send it
support email drafts are shown to the user before sending
the app does not request more permissions than needed for the features included in the current version

rights.title: User rights
rights.body: The user has rights under personal data protection laws to the extent that they apply to the way the app is used.
bullets: the right to access data, rectify it, delete it, and receive a copy of the data
the right to restrict processing and to object to processing based on legitimate interest
the right to withdraw consent at any time where processing is based on consent; withdrawal does not affect the lawfulness of earlier processing
the right to lodge a complaint with the President of the Personal Data Protection Office in Poland (PUODO)
rights.note: Privacy requests can be sent to contact@memicard.com. If data exists only locally on the user's device, some rights may be exercised directly by deleting the data in the app or by removing the app.

hero.title: Licenses and third-party materials
hero.subtitle: This page describes selected third-party materials and the terms under which they are used in the app. It is not part of the privacy policy.

materials.title: Third-party materials
materials.body: Memicard may use selected third-party materials, such as images, illustrations, or other educational assets, in accordance with their license terms or usage rules defined by the rights holders.
bullets: when attribution is required, the app provides the source and basic information about the usage conditions
some materials may be subject to additional limitations, for example regarding logos, trademarks, or presentation style
detailed terms for selected sources may be available under external links

sourceCard.title: Astronomy materials - NOIRLab
sourceCard.body: Some images used in the app come from NOIRLab. Unless otherwise stated for a specific asset, they are used under the Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International license (CC BY 4.0). Source: NOIRLab. The images used in the app have been modified for interface purposes, in particular through cropping, resizing, compression, or other minor visual adjustments. Use of these materials does not imply endorsement or recommendation of the app by NOIRLab.

languageIntro.title: Choose your native language
languageIntro.hintDefault: Choose a language to enable the button.

step1.title: Activate course
step1.description: This is where you activate a course. Games will use flashcards from the active course.

step2.title: Course settings
step2.description: Each course has its own settings that you can change anytime.

step1.title: Hi, I'm Memicard
step1.description: I'll guide you through the app. Let's start by picking courses.

step2.title: Pin a few courses
step2.description: At start, pick courses you care about. You can pin more than one for faster access.

step3.title: After pinning, tap Next
step3.description: Bottom button will enable after you pin at least one course.

step4.title: Custom courses!
step4.description: Later you'll be able to create your own courses with your own flashcards! :3

step1.title: This is the main game
step1.description: Inspired by the Leitner system. This is where we learn words.

step2.title: Game goal
step2.description: Goal is to move a flashcard through all boxes.

step3.title: Activate a box
step3.description: By tapping a box, you activate it and draw a new flashcard.

step4.title: Give an answer
step4.description: A drawn flashcard appears. Depending on type, answer or tap a button.

step5.title: Too complicated?
step5.description: If something is unclear, check the guide on home screen via my top-bar avatar.

step2.title: Choose review mode
step2.description: Start is a quick mini-game session. Traditional is classic translation typing.

step3.title: After session
step3.description: At the end of both modes you'll see a summary table.

step1.title: Choose a course for reviews
step1.description: Tap a tile to open the game and review flashcards from this course.

step2.title: How often do they appear?
step2.description: Maybe in 2 days, maybe in a week, maybe in a year. To remember, first you need to forget ;)

step3.title: What do the numbers mean?
step3.description: Red number means overdue reviews. Green means nothing is waiting now.

step4.title: Disabling reviews
step4.description: You can disable reviews in course settings - then it won't appear on this list.

popups.noRows: No rows can be imported yet.
popups.analyzedSuccess: Great! {{validRows}} rows are ready to import.
popups.templateDirectoryMissing: No directory selected to save the CSV template.
popups.noCardsToImport: There are no cards to import now.

csvGuide.step1: 1. Prepare a CSV or TXT file with column headers.
csvGuide.step2: 2. Pick the file format below and import.
csvGuide.imagesDescription: Next to your CSV or TXT file, create an images folder. Add images there, then put file names in rows, e.g. dog.png. Supported formats: .png, .jpg, .jpeg, .svg.

selfAssess.title: 1) Self-assess
selfAssess.summary: User evaluates: I know it / I don't know it.

traditional.title: 2) Text answer
traditional.summary: Classic flashcard: prompt on front, answer on back.

frontText.description: Card front: question, keyword, or statement.

backText.note: You can enter multiple answers separated by commas or semicolons.

flip.description: If true, card will be flipped during study.

trueFalse.title: 3) True/False
trueFalse.summary: User marks whether the statement is true.

mixed.title: 4) Mixed
mixed.summary: Use multiple flashcard types in one file.

type.note: Values: traditional, true_false, self_assess (plus Polish aliases).

mixed.tip: Column layout stays the same as in sections above.

manualEditor.removeOrCloseImageForCard: Remove image or close image slot for flashcard {{index}}
